What It Is: OEE / downtime / scrap rate / on-time delivery for last 90 days. Becomes the Day 1 performance baseline.
Notes: Anonymized version acceptable. Plant Manager or Ops Head provides.

What It Is: Names and versions of ERP / MES / CMMS / Historian / middleware in use.
Notes: Version numbers required where known.

What It Is: Plant Manager / department leads / decision-making layer. Not the full company org chart.
Notes: Decision-making layer only.

Accelerator Name: Sample Plant Manager Reports or Dashboards
What It Is: Shift reports / daily OEE summaries / MRP exception reports / downtime logs the Plant Manager uses today.
Notes: Any format available.

What It Is: Existing data documentation - even partial or outdated versions are useful.
Notes: Optional. Provide if it exists.

Accelerator Name: MRP Exception Report or Downtime Log Sample
What It Is: Last 30 days of MRP exception messages or downtime records.
Notes: Anonymized if required by security policy.

Accelerator Name: In-Flight Analytics or AI Initiative Documentation
What It Is: Any existing AI / analytics / digital plant initiative docs in progress.
Notes: Prevents IBM from proposing scope already in flight.

What It Is: Fillable template for current OEE / downtime rate / scrap rate / on-time delivery and any other tracked KPIs.
Notes: Sent to Plant Manager or Process Engineer. Used in Day 1 S2 and Day 4 S1.

What It Is: Fillable list of abbreviations / local codes / terms used by shift or line.
Notes: Sent to Plant Manager or Production Supervisor. Used throughout Days 4 and 5.

What It Is: Fillable table: system name / vendor / version / deployment model / primary IT contact / integration docs link.
Notes: Sent to IT or OT Lead. Used in Day 3 S3.

What It Is: Named availability per session per day for all required participants.
Notes: Sent to Workshop Coordinator. Flags scheduling conflicts before Day 1.

What It Is: Session schedule / what each role prepares before arriving / IBM team introductions.
Notes: Sent to all confirmed attendees. Includes process flow reference for Plant Manager.

What It Is: Scope / constraints / timeline / decision-making authority / check-in schedule for executive review.
Notes: Sent to executive team before Day 1. Reflects IBM understanding from pre-work review.

What It Is: Initial list of 8 to 10 candidate decisions derived from client performance report and MRP exception sample.
Notes: Team extends and corrects in session rather than building from blank.

What It Is: One-page: what the workshop produces / what it does not / what IBM needs from the leadership team.
Notes: Presented at start of executive session.

What It Is: Pre-populated with common manufacturing priorities to anchor executive discussion and reduce time to consensus.
Scope / constraints / timeline / decision-making authority / executive check-in schedule. Finalized and signed in session.
Notes: Executive signs in session.

What It Is: Pre-drawn order-to-dispatch flow skeleton that the team annotates in session.
Notes: Plant Manager reviews before Day 1 as part of briefing pack.

What It Is: Common alarm types / downtime reason code structures / scrap and rework logging conventions.
Notes: Helps facilitate exception pattern discussion without relying on client recall.

What It Is: MRP exception categories / inventory mismatch types / cost variance patterns. SAP-specific version if applicable.
Role / five critical operational questions / current answer time per question / decision types owned.
Notes: Used to capture 3 to 5 role profiles during session.

What It Is: Machine breakdown / delayed order / material shortage / capacity crunch. Used to probe exception handling.
Notes: Generic scenarios - not plant-specific. Plant-specific scenario cards built before Day 5.

What It Is: Impact / frequency / urgency / data dependency scoring with pre-scored calibration examples from comparable sites.
Notes: Pre-scored examples anchor the team so discussion starts calibrated.

What It Is: High-impact decisions from comparable manufacturing sites with typical priority scores.
Shortlist / scoring rationale / Day 2 scope proposal structured for a 30-minute executive standing brief.
Notes: IBM completes template during Session 3 before executives return.

What It Is: Impact / frequency / data access / time-to-value scoring pre-populated with Day 1 shortlist and benchmark calibration scores.
Notes: IBM populates with Day 1 shortlist during Day 1 to Day 2 break.

What It Is: A column in the scoring matrix flagging data as confirmed available / likely available / or unknown for each candidate.
Notes: Pulled from Day 1 ERP and MES exception notes.

What It Is: One page per shortlisted use case: scoring summary / known blockers / data dependency status.
Notes: IBM assembles from Session 1 output during morning break.

What It Is: Persona / questions answered / dimensions / time windows / outcome format. Pre-structured for team to fill blanks.
Structured prompt guiding team to articulate a complete example agent response: trigger / data used / recommendation / action enabled.
Notes: Output reviewed and agreed by room in session. Used in Day 4 S2 for trace validation.

What It Is: 30-day / 90-day / 180-day improvement targets over the Day 1 baseline metrics.
Examples of alert / dashboard widget / shift report entry / chat interface with guidance on which suits which user context.
Named fields / their order / visible vs expandable structure for every agent recommendation. The contract between business design and build team.
Approved By: IBM Workshop Lead + Client IT Lead
Notes: Agreed in session. Becomes input to Day 4 S2 decision logic design.

What It Is: Elements that build operator trust: source transparency / confidence indicator / override option / explainability level / comparison to baseline.
Decision type / current approach / changed approach / affected roles / resistance likelihood / mitigation approach.
Responsibilities / time commitment per week / escalation path / feedback loop to IBM during build and rollout.
Notes: Client nominates champion in session. IBM provides the role definition.

What It Is: Pre-listed manufacturing domains with blank owner / steward / and system fields. Team confirms and adjusts.
Which data should travel between systems / common lag and loss points / typical master data misalignment patterns.
Workorder-to-resource / downtime-to-cost / attendance-to-shift join patterns with bridging entity named.
BOMs / routings / UOMs / reason code taxonomy: yes/no assessment sent as pre-work to IT/OT to focus discussion on known problems.
Notes: IT or OT Lead completes before Day 3 S1.

What It Is: Entity name / domain / shared or domain-specific / key attributes / access method / history depth / known quality issues.
Notes: Pre-listed with common manufacturing entities. Team confirms and extends.

What It Is: Per-entity rating: ready / accessible with preparation / or not accessible. Pre-filled by IT/OT before the session.
Notes: IT or OT Lead fills from Day 1 system notes before Day 3.

What It Is: Gap type / blocking / manageable / deferrable classification / resolution approach options / owner / timeline fields.
Common proxy approaches for manufacturing data gaps. Example: using shift calendar when attendance records are incomplete.
Notes: Used when a blocking gap can be bridged with a derived or substitute value.

What It Is: Step-by-step verification: connection string / auth method / test query / result for each in-scope system.
Notes: Run during session where possible. OT network access must be confirmed before Day 3.

What It Is: Reference table of typical record volumes for common manufacturing systems as calibration for client estimates.
Decision tree: data type to sensitivity category to constraint triggered. Covers GDPR / data residency / union / internal security.
Data set / system / format / freshness SLA / sensitivity class / client owner / confirmed access date / IBM acceptance criteria.
Approved By: IBM Workshop Lead + Client IT Lead
Notes: Signed by both parties at end of Day 3 S3. Updated after Day 4 with column-level specifics.

What It Is: Pre-populated with OEE / availability / performance / quality / downtime / cost per unit / throughput / scrap rate.
Notes: Team confirms which apply / adds plant-specific metrics / fills in baseline values from pre-work.

What It Is: Plain-language derivation formulas for OEE decomposition / MTBF / MTTR / scrap rate / cost per unit.
Notes: Validates how the client calculates each metric vs industry-standard formula.

What It Is: Visual metric tree showing compound metrics breaking into sub-metrics and the source fields feeding each node.
Trigger / inputs / rules / thresholds / output recommendation. Pre-structured with use case name and golden sample inserted.
Notes: Built from Day 2 golden sample and Day 2 use case definition output.

What It Is: Common pairs of shop-floor events and the ERP symptoms they cause. Pre-populated for team to validate and extend.
Severity tiers / business impact categories / alert ranking rules when multiple exceptions occur simultaneously.

Accelerator Name: Concept and Filter Rule Definition Template
What It Is: Name / kind (time window or operational filter) / exact filter logic in plain language / source columns it applies to.
Every recommendation type listed. Team marks each as human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop with rationale.
Notes: Signed by Plant Manager and Operations Head in session.

What It Is: Structured walkthrough applying agreed decision logic step by step to the Day 2 golden sample to verify it produces expected output.
Notes: Built from Day 2 golden sample output.

What It Is: Recommendation type / HITL or HOTL / normal operating range / out-of-range approver / emergency approver.
Maps agent recommendation types to ERP records they may trigger with the approval chain for each write-back.
Example agent messages for missing data / stale data / conflicting signals. Team selects and adapts in session.
Notes: Client approves the exact fallback language before build starts.

What It Is: Common signals indicating agent degradation in manufacturing AI deployments with suggested thresholds and response actions.
Pre-structured one-pager: selected use case / design decisions / implementation direction / client commitments / ask for endorsement.
Notes: Drafted during Day 4 S3. Refined Day 5 morning. Presented in Day 5 S3.

What It Is: One card per scenario with realistic plant data values built from client downtime log and MRP exception report.
Notes: Different from Day 1 generic scenario cards. Uses actual plant history provided in pre-work.

What It Is: Expected output / logic output / gap rating (pass or partial or fail) per scenario. Structured for rapid in-session completion.
Each metric linked to its source columns / concepts / and time window filter rules for rapid root cause diagnosis during walkthrough.
Notes: Built from Day 4 metric catalogue output.

What It Is: Known historical exception pairs for testing the accuracy of causal link identification.
Notes: Built from Day 4 correlation map and client plant history provided in pre-work.

What It Is: Gap type / resolution approach / owner / resolution date. Updated in real time during the walkthrough session.
Criteria for high / medium / conditional ratings with minimum scenario pass rate thresholds for each classification.
Pre-listed signals: recommendation acceptance rate / override rate / data freshness status / operator satisfaction with blank threshold fields.
Three common feedback loop patterns: inline override capture / daily summary log / manual review queue with recommendation for plant context.
Override rate thresholds and data quality triggers from comparable manufacturing AI deployments with suggested response actions.
Responsibilities / time commitment per week / escalation path / authority boundaries for the client-side governance owner.
Notes: Client nominates governance owner in session.

What It Is: Document name / owner / review frequency / approved by / process for adding or removing content from agent context.
Approved By: IBM Workshop Lead + Client IT Lead

What It Is: Every workshop output listed with accountable stakeholder / confirmation status / and date. Pre-populated from all days.
Notes: Pre-populated with all named outputs from Days 1 to 5. Stakeholders sign in session.

What It Is: Phase 1 scope / build sequence / client dependency list / success metrics / risk log.
IBM deliverables vs client deliverables side by side with agreed dates from the Day 3 data contract.
Training schedule / rollout sequence / communication approach / adoption champion responsibilities.
Issue description / owner / resolution date / build-phase impact for each open item.
